personal.name: Nikhil Rai
personal.title: Software Engineer & Competitive Programmer
personal.subtitle: Building event-driven architectures, scalable backend systems, and modern web products.
personal.bio: CSE undergraduate with a 9.02 CGPA and a passion for building robust software applications. Active competitive programmer with a 2000+ rating on LeetCode (top 5% globally) and experience in REST APIs, event-driven architectures, and microservices.

education.institution: Noida Institute of Engineering and Technology
education.degree: Bachelor of Technology in Computer Science and Engineering

items.title: Nodebase
items.subtitle: Event-Driven Workflow Automation Platform
items.description: Designed a modular event-driven task orchestration system capable of executing custom multi-step workflows. Implemented microservices-style backend modules with isolated execution contexts and optimized PostgreSQL queries using indexing.
highlights: Reduced manual workflow setup time by ~60%.
Improved system reliability and fault tolerance with isolated backend contexts.
Reduced p95 query latency by ~40% using database indexing.
Integrated Sentry for real-time error tracking, reducing Mean Time to Detection (MTTD) by ~50%.

items.title: WorkNet
items.subtitle: Real-Time Team Communication Platform
items.description: Architected a role-based access control (RBAC) system managing permissions across 5+ channel types. Built real-time messaging and threaded discussions using Convex event-driven architecture.
highlights: Achieved sub-100ms message delivery latency with Convex.
Designed 12+ RESTful backend APIs, cutting average database response time by ~35%.
Modeled normalized schemas to support multi-workspace, multi-channel communication at scale.
Reduced unauthorized access incidents to zero in test scenarios.

items.title: LeetCode 2000+ Rating
items.description: Solved 1000+ algorithmic problems across platforms, ranking in the top 5% globally.

items.title: IICPC CodeFest 2026
items.description: Ranked 2362 among 13,000+ participants globally (Top ~18% in Global Prelims).

items.title: NIET WebWeave Hackathon
items.description: Won 2nd Prize for designing a modern responsive application under time constraints.
